---
title: "Extracting APPGs"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
html <- system("curl -A 'Mozilla/5.0' https://publications.parliament.uk/pa/cm/cmallparty/250618/contents.htm", intern = TRUE)
cat(html, sep = "\n")

library(xml2)
library(rvest)

# Combine lines into a single HTML string
html_text <- paste(html, collapse = "\n")

# Parse into an HTML document
doc <- read_html(html_text)

```

```{r}
library(rvest)

# Assuming 'doc' is your HTML document
links <- html_nodes(doc, "a")            # Find all <a> tags
hrefs <- html_attr(links, "href")        # Extract 'href' attributes

# View the first few hrefs
View(hrefs)
```

```{r}
library(tibble)

# Assuming 'hrefs' is your vector of extracted URLs
url_tibble <- tibble(url = hrefs)

# Preview the result
print(url_tibble)

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)

x <- url_tibble %>% 
   mutate(row_id = row_number()) %>% 
   drop_na()

target_row <- x %>% 
   filter(url == "#subject-groups")
   
target_row_2 <- x %>% 
   filter(url == "http://www.parliament.uk/site-information/azindex/")

foo <- x %>% 
   filter(row_id > target_row$row_id & row_id < target_row_2$row_id)
```

```{r}
base_url <- "https://publications.parliament.uk/pa/cm/cmallparty/250618/"

new_url <- cat(base_url, char(foo$url[1]), sep="")
```

```{r}
df <- data.frame(appg = character())

for (x in 1:length(foo$row_id)) {
   new_row <- data.frame(appg = paste(base_url, char(foo$url[x]), sep=""))
   df <- rbind(df, new_row)
}
```

```{r}
url2 <- pluck(df$appg[1])
cmd <- paste("curl -A 'Mozilla/5.0'", url2)
html <- system(cmd, intern = TRUE)


```
